data.title: Evonik Industries 2Q EPS EUR0.26
Analysts Had Seen Evonik Industries 2Q EPS EUR0.30
Evonik Outlook For 2025: Adjusted EBITDA To Reach Lower End Of Projected Range
data.description:
EVONIK INDUSTRIES AG :

  • FY ADJUSTED EBITDA EUR 2,000-2,300 MILLION

  • ADJUSTED EBITDA DECLINES BY 12 PERCENT IN Q2

  • Q2 NET INCOME AMOUNTED TO EUR 120 MILLION, COMPARED WITH EUR -5 MILLION IN PRIOR-YEAR QUARTER

  • Q2 ADJUSTED EBITDA MARGIN AT 14.5 PERCENT REMAINED AT ABOUT LEVEL OF LAST YEAR (Q2 2024: 14.7 PERCENT)

  • QUARTERLY REVENUE WAS EUR 3.50 BILLION, 11 PERCENT LOWER YEAR-OVER-YEAR (Q2 2024: EUR 3.93 BILLION)

  • OUTLOOK FOR 2025: ADJUSTED EBITDA TO REACH LOWER END OF PROJECTED RANGE

  • STILL EXPECTS CASH CONVERSION RATE TO BE AROUND 40 PERCENT IN 2025

  • OUTLOOK 2025: RETURN ON CAPITAL EMPLOYED IS NOW EXPECTED TO BE IN LINE WITH PREVIOUS YEAR'S LEVEL (2024: 7.1 PERCENT)

  • CAPITAL EXPENDITURES WILL BE REDUCED BY EUR 100 MILLION TO ABOUT EUR 750 MILLION IN 2025

data.title: Fugro lowers full-year core profit margin
data.description:
Dutch geological data specialist Fugro on Friday lowered its core profit margin outlook for the year.

Fugro, which provies geotechnical, survey, subsea and geosciences services, forecast an earnings before interest, taxes margin of between 8% and 11%.

The group previously forecast an annual EBIT margin target of between 11% and 15%.

data.title: Evonik Industries: Unfavorable USD Rates Dragged on Results
Evonik Industries: Results Reflect Tougher Macro Environment in May, June
Australia's Origin Energy rises, brokerages highlight upbeat outlook
data.description:
** Shares of oil and gas retailer Origin Energy rise 1.2% to A$11.81

** Jarden upgrades rating to "Neutral" from "Underweight" and raises price target to A$11.30 from A$10.95

** Brokerage says ORG's qtrly results indicate a decline in upstream production as the Australia Pacific LNG project grapples with strategies to restore output to prior levels

** However, as Jarden's forecasts already account for increased capex and reduced production, the emphasis is on strong momentum developing in energy markets division

** UBS says ORG's "solid" quarterly results de-risk Australia Pacific LNG project production and costs into the medium term

** Stock down ~2% this week, set for its worst week since June 16

data.title: UK Stocks-Factors to watch on August 1
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening lower on Friday, with futures down 0.2%.

* MOTOR FINANCE: The UK's Supreme Court will announce a long-awaited ruling on car finance commissions on Friday that could influence whether major banks face a multibillion-pound compensation bill.

* STEEL: Britain said the European Union will remove tariffs on key steel products under a quota system from Friday as part of a reset of ties and a recent deal to ease trade barriers.

* COMMODITIES: Oil prices were little changed, gold prices traded flat, and copper prices were mixed on Friday.

* FTSE: British equities closed mixed on Thursday, as investors assessed a spate of corporate earnings and looked for potential trade deals before the August 1 tariff deadline expires.

* UK CORPORATE DIARY:

ICAG

Q2 results

Melrose Industries

data.title: Fresenius Medical Care AG expected to post earnings of 47 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Fresenius Medical Care AG , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Bad Homburg Vor Der Hohe Hessen-based company is expected to report a 0.9% increase in revenue to €4.811 billion from €4.77 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 2 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Fresenius Medical Care AG is for earnings of 47 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Fresenius Medical Care AG is $30.00, about 15.5% above its last closing price of $25.34

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

Chinese restaurant operator Green Tea is likely maintain solid performance in the coming years, say Citi analysts in a research note. Green Tea's pricing is accessible and the company emphasizes food quality and safety. Citi believes these factors well position it to expand store openings in various regions across different city tiers in China. Net profit is likely to rise 29% in 2025 and 26% in 2026 on accelerated top-line growth, they say. Citi keeps a buy and high risk rating, with a target price of HK$11.10. Shares are 1.6% higher at HK$7.47.(tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: French and Benelux stocks-Factors to watch
data.description:
Below are company-related news and stories from France and Benelux which could have an impact on the region's markets or individual stocks.

Axa

French insurer Axa nears 1 billion euros deal for Italy's Prima Assicurazioni, Bloomberg reports.

Banijay Group French media production company reported H1 revenue up 6.1% to EUR 2.21 billion and confirmed its 2025 guidance.

Brussel Lambert Belgian investment holding company announced a net asset value of 14.4 billion euros for H1 2025.

Coface SA French credit insurance provider posted H1 net income of EUR 124.2 million, down 12.7% from the previous year.

Euronext Pan-European stock exchange operator delivered Q2 revenue of EUR 465.8 million, beating consensus estimates.

Teleperformance French customer experience management firm reported Q2 revenue of EUR 2.50 billion but lowered its 2025 growth outlook.

Universal Music Group Dutch-American music corporation matched expectations with H1 revenue of EUR 5,881 million and announced an interim dividend.

Viridien SA French energy services company increased Q2 revenue by 6% to $274 million and extended its bond maturity to 2030.

Vivendi SE French media conglomerate recorded H1 earnings of €30 million for shareholders, reducing net debt to €1,768 million.
